page: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 64 physical AMD EPYC-Turin cores on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates top-tier performance across memory and processing workloads. It achieves a Passmark CPU score of 98,751.74 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 117,958.63. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 533.09 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 205.9 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Additionally, the processor's support for advanced vector extensions enables strong performance in LLM inference, processing 198.97 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

Because the CPU allocation is shared, there may be resource contention under sustained, continuous peak loads compared to dedicated instances. However, the high density of 512.0 GB RAM and 3840 GB NVMe SSD storage provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require constant 100% dedicated CPU execution. This hardware profile is highly suited for large-scale database operations, Redis deployments, memory-cached web serving, and data processing pipelines.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 64 shared AMD EPYC-Turin physical cores on the x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmarks show top-tier capabilities, including a Passmark CPU score of 98,751.74 and a large-block memory read bandwidth of 533.09 GB/sec. The server also performs strongly in database operations and LLM inference, processing 76.47 tokens per second for text generation on a medium 7B model. While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained peak compute workloads, the high memory-to-core ratio and fast local storage deliver excellent resource density. This configuration is well-suited for large databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 shared AMD EPYC-Turin cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512g-3840s is a memory-optimized server with 64 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe storage for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC cores and fast NVMe storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 shared AMD EPYC-Turin physical cores running at 2.0 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
3840 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory read bandwidth of 533.09 GB/sec
Strong performance in database operations and Redis deployments
Suited for in-memory caching, large databases, and data processing
